name: Backward-Facing Step (Pitz-Daily)
description: Turbulent flow over a backward-facing step. A classic validation case for turbulence models — flow separates at the step and reattaches downstream.
learning_objectives: Set up a steady-state RANS simulation
Configure inlet velocity and turbulence boundary conditions
Understand the SIMPLE pressure-velocity coupling algorithm

mesh.title: Generate Mesh
mesh.description: Create the step geometry mesh using blockMesh. The domain includes an inlet channel, a backward-facing step, and a downstream recovery region.
mesh.tip: The mesh is finer near the step where flow separation occurs.

boundaries.title: Set Boundary Conditions
boundaries.description: Configure velocity, pressure, and turbulence quantities (k, epsilon) at the inlet, outlet, and walls.
boundaries.tip: The inlet uses fixedValue for U and calculated values for k and epsilon based on turbulence intensity.

solver.title: Configure Solver
solver.description: Set up simpleFoam with SIMPLE algorithm settings. This is a steady-state solver — it iterates until residuals converge.
solver.tip: For steady-state, 'endTime' is the maximum number of iterations, not physical time.

run.title: Run Simulation
run.description: Execute simpleFoam. Watch residuals — all fields should decrease below 1e-4 for a converged solution.
run.tip: If residuals oscillate, try reducing relaxation factors in fvSolution.

postprocess.title: View Results
postprocess.description: Visualize the velocity field to see flow separation behind the step and the reattachment point downstream.
postprocess.tip: In ParaView, use a streamline or vector plot to see the recirculation zone behind the step.
